_note: COLD-START BOOTSTRAP SEED — NOT the final design. Geopolitical relationships are dynamic (alliances shift, proxy control weakens, new conflicts emerge); this file exists only because the memory system starts empty and GRI needs SOME relationship knowledge to produce root_cause_groups on day one. 2 of 3 relationships are physical geography (chokepoint chains — genuinely static); the Iran-proxy link is a current-era geopolitical prior that WILL become stale if the political landscape changes. The GRI prompt instructs 'override if evidence contradicts' so this seed cannot force incorrect groupings. Target: replace with memory-driven dynamic relationships (Phase 1) + knowledge graph (Phase 2) — design doc: docs/dynamic_corridor_relationships.md. Until then this file is the scaffold, not the building.

relationships.mechanism: Iran-backed Houthi militia controls Bab el-Mandeb access; Suez Canal traffic disrupted when Red Sea is unsafe. Hormuz escalation by Iran frequently co-occurs with Houthi Red Sea attacks as coordinated pressure.
sources: UN Panel of Experts on Yemen

relationships.mechanism: Bab el-Mandeb closure blocks the southern Suez approach; traffic reroutes around Africa via Cape of Good Hope, overloading that route.
Suez closure forces Europe-Asia traffic around the Cape of Good Hope (+14 days, freight multiplier ~1.35x).
